source: Synthetic — marketing email that trips the keyword backstop ('book your appointment', 'schedule') without containing a real time-bound commitment. Models the SKIP safety valve.

input.subject: Book your appointment today — limited slots available!
input.bodyTruncated:
Hi Rebekah,

It's been a while since your last visit! Book your appointment today and receive a complimentary whitening consultation.

Our calendar is open for cleanings, exams, and cosmetic consultations all spring. Click the button below to schedule a time that works for you.

[BOOK NOW]

We also offer evening and Saturday appointments. Don't wait — our spring schedule fills up fast!

Brilliant Smiles Dental
123 Main St, Clermont, FL 34711

notes: Marketing CTA false positive — keywords 'book your appointment' / 'schedule' tripped the reconciliation pre-filter, and the extractor hallucinated a startISO (or the email's send-time was promoted to a candidate interval). The daySchedule happens to contain a 6 PM 'Family dinner' that overlaps the hallucinated interval, so the arbiter IS called. The arbiter should recognize the body as generic marketing (no specific committed time slot, CTA button instead of a concrete appointment) and return SKIP. This is the safety-valve test: the SEPARATE default would create a spurious 'Book appointment' event on the calendar; SKIP is the correct outcome per D-06.
